source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Flash fire at crane engine air intake
actions_taken: Helicopter transport arranged to Sabine shore base
Injured person transported to Prime Occupational Medical Clinic in Sulfur, Louisiana
BSEE investigation conducted with interviews and witness statements
event.summary: On 11-April-2018 at 17:15 hours, a flash fire occurred at the crane engine air intake blower during fuel system bleed-down operations at platform HI 22 A. Two Shamrock Energy Solution Mechanics were performing crane engine overhaul. After exhausting stored air pressure, facility Operators opened a valve sending natural gas to the fuel gas system to supply additional pressure for the starter. On the third cranking of the starter, a flash fire occurred at the engine air intake blower. One Mechanic sustained 1st and 2nd degree burns to hands, arms, face, and ears. The root cause was failure to vent the crane engine starter discharge to a safe area, combined with lack of communication that the starting system utilized natural gas instead of compressed air, and absence of a Management of Change procedure when the facility's air system was modified to a gas system.
recommendations: Office of Safety Management to evaluate the incident
Safety Alert when using natural gas versus air

hazards.name: Pressurized natural gas in crane engine starting system
Flammable gas-air mixture at engine intake

threats.name: Uncontrolled discharge of natural gas from starter into engine air intake
Mechanics unaware of natural gas system configuration

consequences.name: Flash fire at engine air intake blower

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for crane engine starting system
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures for work activities
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify hazards before work begins

supporting_text: The crane engine starting system utilizing natural gas was not covered in the Job Safety Analysis (JSA)

controls.name: Communication of system configuration to mechanics
controls.barrier_role: Ensure workers are informed of hazardous system configurations

supporting_text: The Operators stated they did not inform the two Mechanics that the starting system was configured with flammable natural gas
Facility Operators failed to inform the Mechanics working on crane engine that the starting system utilized natural gas instead of compressed air for its actuation

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) procedure for system modifications
controls.barrier_role: Identify and control hazards when equipment or procedures are modified
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control for managing facility changes

supporting_text: The Lessee modified the facility's Utility and Instrument Air system to an Instrument Gas system on 20-November-2017. This action was completed without Lessee preparing and completing a Management of Change (MOC) to identify and control hazards associated with equipment and facility operating procedures

Lessee modified the facility's Utility and Instrument Air system to an Instrument Gas system without completing a Management of Change (MOC). The Lessee should have identified the need for the starter to be vented to a safe area, when completing the MOC

controls.name: Proper venting of crane engine starter discharge
controls.barrier_role: Direct starter discharge away from ignition sources and engine air intake
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to prevent gas accumulation near ignition sources

supporting_text: Lessee failed to ensure the discharge of the crane engine starter was vented to a safe area. Discharge was found to vent against engine block on the same side as engine air intake and blower
The Mechanics also stated they would not have operated the starter with gas due to the starter's discharge not being properly vented away from the engine

controls.name: Pressure Safety Valve (PSV) on volume tank
controls.barrier_role: Limit pressure in volume tank to safe operating limits

supporting_text: The PSV was set at 275 psi, according to Lessee's test records. The Maximum Allowable Working Pressure (MAWP) of the crane engine starter is only 150 psi

controls.name: Temperature Safety Element (TSE) on volume tank
controls.barrier_role: Protect against overpressurization due to temperature increase
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control for pressure vessel safety

supporting_text: By connecting the volume tank to the fuel gas system, the vessel became subject to API 14 C evaluation and the tank was not equipped with a temperature safety element (TSE)

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
